title: Antimicrobial inserts for stopcock medical connectors
abstract: Various embodiments of an antimicrobial insert for a stopcock medical connector are provided. More specifically, the present invention relates to an antimicrobial insert that is seated within at least a portion of the annular bore of the connector's tap, wherein fluid within the annular bore contacts the antimicrobial insert, thereby preventing microbial proliferation within the stopcock medical connector.
claims:
Claims 1. An antimicrobial insert device for placement within a fluid pathway of a stopcock tap. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the antimicrobial insert is comprised of an antimicrobial material. 3. The device of claim 1, wherein the antimicrobial insert is coated with an antimicrobial material. 4. The device of claim 1, comprising an annulus shape having an outer circumference defining an outer surface, and an inner circumference defining a fluid pathway through the antimicrobial insert. 5. The device of claim 4, wherein the outer circumference is selected to compatible seat the antimicrobial insert within the fluid pathway of the stopcock tap. 6. The device of claim 5, wherein the fluid pathway of the stopcock tap comprises an annular bore. 7. The device of claim 1, comprising a three-dimensional matrix forming a frame structure and having a plurality of apertures. 8. The device of claim 7, wherein the insert is comprised of an antimicrobial material. 9. The device of claim 7, wherein the insert is coated with an antimicrobial material. 10. The device of claim 1, further comprising an antimicrobial composition that either comprises or coats the insert, wherein the antimicrobial composition is selected from the group consisting of chlorhexidine diacetate, chlorhexidine gluconate, alexidine, silver sulfadiazine, silver acetate, silver citrate hydrate, cetrimide, cetyl pyridium chloride, benzalkonium chloride, o-phthalaldehyde, and silver element. 11. A stopcock medical connector device, comprising: a housing having a proximal opening, a distal end, and an inner lumen extending therebetween, the housing further comprising a plurality of side ports extending outwardly from the inner lumen, each side port having a hollow interior in fluid communication with the inner lumen; a tap rotatably seated in the inner lumen of the housing and comprising an annular bore forming a fluid pathway through a shaft portion of the tap, wherein the tap is rotated within the inner lumen to align the plurality of side ports with the annular bore to provide fluid communication between the plurality of side ports and the annular bore; and an antimicrobial insert seated within the annular bore in proximity to the inner lumen. 12. The device of claim 11, wherein the antimicrobial insert comprises an annulus shape having an outer circumference defining an outer surface, and an inner circumference defines 

 a fluid pathway through the antimicrobial insert, wherein the annular bore is in fluid communication with the plurality of side ports via the fluid pathway of the antimicrobial insert. 13. The device of claim 11, wherein the plurality of side ports each comprise an inner diameter that is less than the outer circumference, and greater than the inner circumference of the antimicrobial insert. 14. The device of claim 13, further comprising a plurality of annular bores. 15. The device of claim 14, further comprising a plurality of antimicrobial inserts. 16. The device of claim 11, wherein the antimicrobial comprises a three-dimensional matrix forming a frame structure having a plurality of apertures. 17. The device of claim 16, wherein the insert is comprised of an antimicrobial material. 18. The device of claim 16, wherein the insert is coated with an antimicrobial material. 19. A method for manufacturing a stopcock medical connector device, the method comprising steps for: providing a housing having a proximal opening, a distal end, and an inner lumen extending therebetween, the housing further comprising a plurality of side ports extending outwardly from the inner lumen, each side port having a hollow interior in fluid communication with the inner lumen; rotatably seating a tap in the inner lumen of the housing, the tap comprising an annular bore forming a fluid pathway through a shaft portion of the tap, wherein the tap is rotated within the inner lumen to align the plurality of side ports with the annular bore to provide fluid communication between the plurality of side ports and the annular bore; and inserting an antimicrobial insert within the annular bore and in proximity to the inner lumen. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the antimicrobial insert comprises an annulus shape having an outer circumference defining an outer surface, and an inner circumference defines a fluid pathway through the antimicrobial insert, wherein the annular bore is in fluid communication with the plurality of side ports via the fluid pathway of the antimicrobial insert.
